raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing a charge of {$2400.00} related to a hotel reservation made through a third-party travel service. The hotels General Manager has confirmed in writing that a full refund was issued to the third-party company. Despite this, the third-party company has refused to process the refund and continues to claim they were unable to reach the hotel a claim the hotel has explicitly denied in writing. 

I submitted this written confirmation from the hotel, along with a XXXX balance receipt and other supporting documentation, to my credit card issuer during the dispute process. The initial dispute was denied. I then escalated the matter to the Office of the President, and a second investigation was conducted. Despite the evidence provided, the dispute was again denied.

The card issuer stated the denial was based in part on a statement I allegedly made acknowledging the reservation, but they have refused to provide any written record or transcript to support this claim when I formally requested it. 

The hotel is not in possession of the funds. They clearly stated the refund was sent to the third party, who is now wrongfully withholding it. The credit card company has failed to take appropriate action despite receiving clear documentation showing the funds were no longer with the hotel. 

I am requesting that the charge be reversed and a refund issued to me. I have included the hotels email confirmation, the third-partys response, and all relevant correspondence to support this complaint.
